title: Electrospun Silk-Boron Nitride Nanofibers with Tunable Structure and Properties
subtitle: Polymers 2020

slides.title: Electrospun Silk-Boron Nitride Nanofibers with Tunable Structure and Properties
slides.subtitle: Polymers 2020
slides.note: MDPI abstract, article page, figures 1-5 and conclusion.

cards.title: Why it matters
cards.text: Electrospun silk can be made into porous mats, but its thermal and structural stability still needs help.

cards.title: Main bottleneck
cards.text: A filler must reinforce the protein matrix without destroying processability.

cards.title: Core idea
cards.text: Use h-BN nanosheets to tune fiber morphology, crosslinking and protein secondary structure.

items.title: Electrospinning matures
items.desc: Fiber morphology becomes controllable by process tuning.

items.title: Silk biomaterials
items.desc: Silk expands into scaffolds, dressings and devices.

items.title: BN thermal fillers
items.desc: BN becomes a major electrically insulating conductor filler.

items.title: Silk-BN fibers
items.desc: The paper maps how BN changes fiber structure and stability.

steps.title: Dissolve silk
steps.desc: Prepare a formic-acid based spinning dope.

steps.title: Add BN
steps.desc: Mix h-BN at different loading ratios.

steps.title: Electrospin
steps.desc: Form composite fibers with tunable diameter and packing.

steps.title: Characterize
steps.desc: Use SEM, FTIR, TGA and TM-DSC to map structure-property changes.

slides.title: 核心公式与机理
slides.formula: Thermal stability is tracked by Tw, Tg and Td shifts after BN addition.

stats.label: approx. diameter of pure SF fibers

cards.title: Method validation
cards.text: Structure, thermal and cooling behavior are all checked.

cards.title: Indoor tests
cards.text: Simulated skin and wearable scenarios are used.

cards.title: Outdoor tests
cards.text: Sunlight or environment are incorporated when relevant.

cards.title: Stability
cards.text: Cycle, wetting or degradation are quantified.

cards.title: Heat transfer
cards.text: The filler network changes phonon pathways and heat spreading.

cards.title: Interface control
cards.text: Surface chemistry decides whether the network remains useful.

rows: approx. diameter of pure SF fibers

cards.title: Skin contact
cards.text: Wearable patches and body surface cooling.

cards.title: Bioelectronics
cards.text: Heat removal from LEDs or soft devices.

cards.title: Reusable settings
cards.text: Washing, cycling or reuse depending on the paper.

slides.title: 局限与展望
limitations: The paper emphasizes structure tuning more than conductivity benchmarking.
Higher loading can roughen the fibers.
futureWork: Measure conductivity and anisotropy explicitly in aligned mats.
Link morphology tuning to cell response in wet environments.
Explore post-spinning annealing or stretching to amplify ordering.
Combine BN with additional benign fillers for multifunctionality.

items.text: h-BN changes the morphology, secondary structure and thermal stability of electrospun silk fibers, making them more tunable for biofriendly composite use.
The main design principle is to align filler geometry, interfacial chemistry and matrix choice with the target cooling mode.
